hook: You already know how to solve linear equations like $2x + 3 = 7$, but what happens when a squared term like $x^2$ enters the picture? Linear methods break down, and a whole new toolkit is needed. By the end of this unit, you'll have multiple powerful strategies to crack open any quadratic equation — and you'll know exactly which one to reach for.
concept: Every topic in this unit is a different key to the same lock: finding the values of $x$ that make a quadratic equation true. Together, they give you a complete, flexible approach to solving any equation of the form $ax^2 + bx + c = 0$.

topic_roadmap.description: You will learn to isolate a squared expression and undo it with a square root, solving equations like $3x^2 = 75$ quickly and directly.
Master the technique of rewriting any quadratic as a perfect square trinomial so it can be solved with square roots, even when factoring isn't obvious.
Apply the formula $x = \dfrac{-b \pm \sqrt{b^2 - 4ac}}{2a}$ to solve any quadratic equation by simply identifying and substituting the coefficients $a$, $b$, and $c$.
You will learn to evaluate $b^2 - 4ac$ to predict — before solving — whether a quadratic has two real solutions, one real solution, or no real solutions.
Develop the judgment to look at any quadratic equation and select the fastest, most efficient method — square roots, factoring, completing the square, or the quadratic formula.
Apply your full toolkit to real-world problems involving projectile motion, area, and other contexts where quadratic equations model what's actually happening.
